description: ISessionLifecycle.open() is never called during SessionDispatcher.dispatch() or SessionRegistry.register(). Sessions are created without lifecycle activation. lifecycle.close() is called indirectly via registry.unregister() -> _on_session_end() -> coordinator.close() but there is no direct open() path from the delivery path.
proposed_solution: Inject ISessionLifecycle into SessionRegistry or SessionDispatcher; call .open() with SessionConfig during register() and .refresh() per message

description: Three complete adapter implementations exist (CLISessionAdapter / InProcessSessionAdapter / NetworkSessionAdapter) but assembler.py's _build_session_registry() does not pass checkpoint_sync to SessionRegistry. persist() will never be called in production despite ready adapters.
proposed_solution: Pass checkpoint_sync to _build_session_registry() in assembler.py using appropriate adapter per transport_type

description: SessionRegistry.evict_idle() only checks idle_timeout (last_active threshold) and never checks ttl_seconds (created_at + ttl). Sessions past their absolute TTL remain indefinitely. Default eviction_interval of 60s adds delay.
proposed_solution: Extend evict_idle() with TTL condition: entry.created_at + entry.ttl < now. Reduce eviction_interval to 10-30s

description: SessionRegistry and SessionDispatcher are purely local using in-memory dict. Sessions cannot be shared across multiple fabric instances. Horizontal scaling impossible.
proposed_solution: Add RedisSessionRegistry and RedisSessionDispatcher persisting session state in Redis with pickle/json serialization

description: No unit or integration tests exist for any session component: SessionRegistry
column13: Create comprehensive test suite: (1) register/dispatch/resolve (2) failover with frozen DispatchContext (3) coordinator.handle_success/failure (4) full adapter lifecycle (5) eviction idle + TTL

description: SessionRegistry._on_session_end() never calls checkpoint.prune(). Some adapters implement prune() (CLI calls it in close()
severity: InProcess supports it) but no auto-prune or manual trigger exists from the orchestration layer. Checkpoints accumulate without limit.
belong_to_existing_file: Add checkpoint.prune() call inside _on_session_end() after persist(). Add scheduled prune task in SessionRegistry per PrunePolicy

component: TTL Expiry Not Checked in EvictIdle (dup SES-003)
description: SessionRegistry.evict_idle() checks only idle_timeout. Sessions with recent activity but total age exceeding TTL are never evicted. default_ttl=3600 is completely ineffective.
proposed_solution: Add TTL condition to evict_idle(): now - entry.created_at > entry.ttl. Merge both conditions in same pass

description: assembler.py _build_session_registry() does not pass checkpoint_sync to SessionRegistry despite session_provider factory fully supporting it. _checkpoint_sync remains None in production.
proposed_solution: Pass checkpoint_sync to _build_session_registry() using SessionFactory to create appropriate adapter per transport_type

component: Lifecycle.open() Not Called in Register (dup SES-001)
description: SessionRegistry.register() creates SessionEntry directly without calling lifecycle.open(). SessionConfig (ttl_seconds
dependencies: metadata) is never passed to ISessionLifecycle. SessionConfig is useless.
file_ID: Inject ISessionLifecycle into SessionRegistry and call lifecycle.open(config) inside register() before creating SessionEntry

dependencies: or retry hooks. Leaves 5 critical bugs (SES-007/008/009/010/013) undetected.
